claim.content: It is conjectured that superconductivity observed in magic-angle twisted bilayer graphene at zero magnetic field, which typically emerges upon doping correlated insulating states, may also reappear (be re-entrant) at magnetic flux $\phi=2\pi$ per moiré unit cell when correlated insulating states re-emerge and interaction-driven Fermi-surface reconstructions occur at that flux; this statement is explicitly a conjecture and is not established by the single-particle or many-body calculations presented here.
